# ADR-NNN: Short title in imperative form

<!-- Replace "ADR-NNN" with the next sequential three-digit number (e.g., ADR-006).
     The title must be a short imperative phrase describing the decision.
     Example: "ADR-006: Use Markdown for all documentation artifacts" -->

## Status

<!-- Choose exactly one of the following statuses and delete the rest:
     - Proposed           — under discussion, not yet decided
     - Accepted           — the decision has been made and is in effect
     - Accepted (retroactive) — the decision predates the ADR system and is recorded retroactively
     - Deprecated         — the decision is no longer recommended but still in place
     - Superseded by ADR-NNN — this ADR has been replaced; update NNN with the superseding ADR number -->

Proposed

## Context

<!-- Describe the forces at play that made this decision necessary.
     Answer: What is the situation? What constraints, goals, or tensions exist?
     What alternatives were available? What would happen without this decision?
     Write in neutral, factual prose — do not explain the decision here, only the context that led to it. -->

## Decision

<!-- State the decision in active voice starting with "We will..." or "The system uses...".
     Be specific. This section should be unambiguous enough that a new contributor
     can understand exactly what was decided without reading any other document.
     Example: "We will store all SDD artifacts in Engram using topic keys under sdd/{change-name}/
     so that decisions and plans are version-controlled alongside the configuration." -->

We will ...

## Consequences

<!-- List the trade-offs introduced by this decision — both positive and negative.
     Positive consequences: what becomes easier, more consistent, or more maintainable.
     Negative consequences: what becomes harder, what new constraints are introduced.
     Example:
     **Positive:**
     - Skills are self-contained and co-locate all related files
     - Claude can locate any skill without directory scanning

     **Negative:**
     - Requires strict naming discipline — all entry points must be named SKILL.md
     - Slightly more ceremony for single-file skills that do not need a directory -->

**Positive:**

- <!-- Benefit 1 -->

**Negative:**
